description: CLI interface for Notion API with database query filtering

auth.usage_instructions: Run `notion auth --help` for exact arguments and options.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.
login.usage_instructions: Run `notion auth login --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

options.name: --force
options.help: Clear existing ephemeral auth state and re-authenticate

logout.help: Clear stored credentials and browser sessions.
logout.usage_instructions: Run `notion auth logout --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

profiles.usage_instructions: Run `notion auth profiles --help` for exact arguments and options.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Run `notion auth profiles create --help` for exact arguments and options.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Run `notion auth profiles delete --help` for exact arguments and options.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.
get.usage_instructions: Run `notion auth profiles get --help` for exact arguments and options.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.
list.usage_instructions: Run `notion auth profiles list --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

remove.help: Delete a profile and its data.

select.help: Activate a profile within its auth type.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to activate within its auth type

status.usage_instructions: Run `notion auth status --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Run `notion auth test --help` for exact arguments and options.

cache.usage_instructions: Run `notion cache --help` for exact arguments and options.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Run `notion cache clear --help` for exact arguments and options.

comments.help: Manage comments on pages and blocks
comments.usage_instructions: Run `notion comments --help` for exact arguments and options.

create.help: Create a comment on a page, block, or in a discussion thread.
examples: notion comments create "This is my comment" --page-id abc123
notion comments create "Comment on this block" --block-id def456
create.usage_instructions: Run `notion comments create --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --page-id
options.help: Page ID to add comment to

options.name: --block-id
options.help: Block ID to add comment to

options.name: --discussion-id
options.help: Discussion thread ID to reply to

get.help: Get a specific comment by ID.
get.usage_instructions: Run `notion comments get --help` for exact arguments and options.

list.help: List comments on a page, block, or in a discussion thread.
list.usage_instructions: Run `notion comments list --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --page-id
options.help: Page ID to get comments for

options.name: --block-id
options.help: Block ID to get comments for

options.name: --discussion-id
options.help: Discussion thread ID to get comments for

options.name: --with-context
options.help: Include parent block text as context (only with --page-id)

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of comments to return

options.name: --max-workers
options.help: Maximum concurrent block comment lookups when using --with-context

database.usage_instructions: Run `notion database --help` for exact arguments and options.

create.help: Create a new database under a parent page.

arguments.name: parent_page_id
arguments.help: The parent page ID the new database is created under. Database and data_source IDs are not valid parents

options.name: --title-property
options.help: Name of the title property to create (Notion requires exactly one)

options.name: --inline
options.help: Create the database inline in the parent page

options.name: --properties
options.help: Raw JSON for initial_data_source.properties (Notion API format). Merged with convenience flags; a title property is added if none is present

options.name: --select
options.help: Add a select property (format: 'Name' or 'Name:Opt1|Opt2'). Repeatable

options.name: --multi-select
options.help: Add a multi_select property (format: 'Name' or 'Name:Opt1|Opt2'). Repeatable

options.name: --status
options.help: Add a status property (format: 'Name' or 'Name:Opt1|Opt2'). Repeatable

options.name: --relation
options.help: Add a relation property (format: 'Name:target_data_source_id'). Uses the TARGET's data_source_id. Repeatable

options.name: --relation-type
options.help: Relation type for --relation properties (dual_property or single_property)

get.usage_instructions: Run `notion database get --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --data-source
options.help: Specific data_source ID to use when the database container holds multiple data sources

list.usage_instructions: Run `notion database list --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --sort
options.help: Sort direction by last edited time (asc/desc)

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of results to return

options.name: --filter
options.help: Filter using field:op:value format (e.g., title:like:%project%). Client-side filtering

page.usage_instructions: Run `notion database page --help` for exact arguments and options.

content.usage_instructions: Run `notion database page content --help` for exact arguments and options.

append.help: Append content blocks to a page. Automatically handles content larger than Notion's 100-block limit by splitting into chunks and uploading sequentially.
examples: notion database page content append PAGE_ID --text "Hello world"
notion database page content append PAGE_ID --text "# Heading\n\nParagraph text"
notion database page content append PAGE_ID --file content.md
notion database page content append PAGE_ID --paragraph "Simple paragraph"
append.usage_instructions: Run `notion database page content append --help` for exact arguments and options.

arguments.name: page_id
arguments.help: The page ID to append content to

options.name: --file
options.help: File containing text/markdown content to append

clear.help: Clear all content from a page.
examples: notion database page content clear PAGE_ID
notion database page content clear PAGE_ID --force
clear.usage_instructions: Run `notion database page content clear --help` for exact arguments and options.

arguments.name: page_id
arguments.help: The page ID to clear content from

list-blocks.help: List a page's content blocks with their block IDs, types, and text.

arguments.name: page_id
arguments.help: The page ID to list content blocks for

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of top-level blocks to return (default: all blocks)

set.help: Replace all page content with new content. This clears existing content and sets new content. Automatically handles content larger than Notion's 100-block limit by splitting into chunks and uploading sequentially. Use --json-file to import raw Notion blocks (preserving rich formatting like callouts, colors, columns) exported via 'notion pages export --format notion-json'.
examples: notion database page content set PAGE_ID --text "New content"
notion database page content set PAGE_ID --file content.md
notion database page content set PAGE_ID --json-file blocks.json
set.usage_instructions: Run `notion database page content set --help` for exact arguments and options.

arguments.name: page_id
arguments.help: The page ID to set content for

options.name: --file
options.help: File containing text/markdown content to set

options.name: --json-file
options.help: File containing Notion JSON blocks to set (from 'export --format notion-json')

update-block.help: Edit a single existing block's text IN PLACE without deleting it.

options.name: --block-id
options.help: The block ID to update in place

options.name: --text
options.help: New plain-text content for the block (replaces existing text)

options.name: --checked
options.help: Set the checked state for a to_do block

options.name: --table
options.help: Display the updated block as a formatted table

create.help: Create a new page in a database.
examples: notion database page create DB_ID --title "My Page"
notion database page create DB_ID --title "My Page" --status "In progress"
notion database page create DB_ID --title "My Page" --select "Client:Acme Corp"
notion database page create DB_ID --title "My Page" --content-file content.md
notion database page create DB_ID --title "My Page" --blocks-file blocks.json
notion database page create DB_ID --title "My Page" --from-template default
notion database page create DB_ID --title "My Page" --from-template TEMPLATE_ID
notion database page create DB_ID --title "My Page" --properties '{"Priority": {"select": {"name": "High"}}}'
create.usage_instructions: Run `notion database page create --help` for exact arguments and options.

arguments.name: database_id
arguments.help: The database ID to create the page in

options.name: --title
options.help: Page title (auto-detects the database's title property)

options.name: --content-file
options.help: File containing markdown content for the page body

options.name: --blocks-file
options.help: File containing Notion JSON blocks (from 'export --format notion-json')

options.name: --from-template
options.help: Create page from template (use template ID or 'default' for default template)

options.name: --properties
options.help: Raw JSON properties object (Notion API format)

delete.help: Delete (archive) a page. Note: Notion doesn't truly delete pages - this archives them. Archived pages can be restored using 'notion database page update PAGE_ID --restore'.
delete.usage_instructions: Run `notion database page delete --help` for exact arguments and options.

get.help: Get a specific page by ID.
examples: notion database page get abc123-def456 --include-blocks --markdown --out-file content.md
get.usage_instructions: Run `notion database page get --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --include-blocks
options.help: Include page content blocks in output

list.help: List pages in a database with optional filtering. Filter Examples: notion database page list DB_ID --filter "Status:eq:Done" notion database page list DB_ID --filter "Status:In progress"  # eq is default notion database page list DB_ID --filter "Priority:eq:High" --filter "Status:ne:Archived" notion database page list DB_ID --filter "Complete:true" notion database page list DB_ID --filter "Name:like:%project%" Output Examples: notion database page list DB_ID --table notion database page list DB_ID --properties "Title,Status,Due Date" notion database page list DB_ID --limit 10
list.usage_instructions: Run `notion database page list --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --filter
options.help: Filter using field:op:value format (e.g., Status:eq:Done, Priority:like:%High%). Can be used multiple times

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of properties to include in output

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of results to return

options.name: --data-source
options.help: Specific data_source ID when the database container holds multiple data sources

update.help: Update a page's properties. Property Examples: notion database page update PAGE_ID --status "Done" notion database page update PAGE_ID --status "Status:In progress" notion database page update PAGE_ID --select "Priority:High" notion database page update PAGE_ID --select "Client:Acme" --select "Contact:Jane Doe" notion database page update PAGE_ID --text "Notes:Updated content" notion database page update PAGE_ID --checkbox "Complete:true" notion database page update PAGE_ID --number "Score:95" Archive/Restore: notion database page update PAGE_ID --archive notion database page update PAGE_ID --restore Raw JSON (for complex updates): notion database page update PAGE_ID --properties '{"Status": {"status": {"name": "Done"}}}'
update.usage_instructions: Run `notion database page update --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --properties
options.help: Raw JSON properties object (Notion API format)

add.help: Add a new field (property) to a database.
examples: notion field add DB_ID "Due Date" --type date
notion field add DB_ID "Score" --type number --number-format percent
notion field add DB_ID "Related Tasks" --type relation --relation-database OTHER_DB_ID

options.help: Maximum number of fields to return

add.usage_instructions: Run `notion field option add --help` for exact arguments and options.

examples: notion field rename DB_ID "Old Name" "New Name"

options.name: --relation-database
options.help: For relation fields: the TARGET's database container ID OR data_source ID. Resolved to the target's data_source_id (API 2025-09-03)

options.help: Relation type for relation fields (dual_property or single_property)

pages.usage_instructions: Run `notion pages --help` for exact arguments and options.

blocks.usage_instructions: Run `notion pages blocks --help` for exact arguments and options.

append.help: Append child blocks to a block or page. Automatically handles content larger than Notion's 100-block limit by splitting into chunks and uploading sequentially.
examples: notion pages blocks append BLOCK_ID --text "New paragraph"
notion pages blocks append BLOCK_ID --file content.md
notion pages blocks append PAGE_ID --after BLOCK_ID --text "Inserted after specific block"
notion pages blocks append PAGE_ID --file chapter.md --is-toggleable
append.usage_instructions: Run `notion pages blocks append --help` for exact arguments and options.

arguments.name: block_id
arguments.help: The block or page ID to append children to

options.name: --text
options.help: Text/markdown content to append as child blocks

options.name: --file
options.help: File containing text/markdown content to append

options.name: --json
options.help: Raw JSON array of blocks to append (advanced)

options.name: --json-file
options.help: File containing raw JSON array of blocks to append (advanced)

options.name: --after
options.help: Block ID to insert after (for positioning within parent's children)

options.name: --is-toggleable
options.help: Make all heading_1/2/3 blocks generated from markdown into toggle headings (markdown input only; ignored with --json/--json-file)

options.name: --synced
options.help: Wrap the supplied content in one original synced block

options.name: --synced-from
options.help: Create a duplicate synced block from the original synced block ID

delete.help: Delete a block. Note: Deleted blocks are moved to trash and can be recovered via Notion UI.
delete.usage_instructions: Run `notion pages blocks delete --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --recursive
options.help: Delete all children blocks first (in parallel)

get.help: Get a specific block by ID.
get.usage_instructions: Run `notion pages blocks get --help` for exact arguments and options.

list.help: List blocks (children) of a page or block.
list.usage_instructions: Run `notion pages blocks list --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --page-id
options.help: The page or block ID to list children of

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of blocks to return (default: all blocks)

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include in output

update.help: Update a block's content. For simple text updates, use --text. For complex updates, use --json or --json-file. Use --toggleable / --no-toggleable to flip the is_toggleable flag on existing heading_1/2/3 blocks. The flag can be combined with --text to rename and toggle in a single call. By default, --toggleable also re-parents the heading's section siblings (everything from the heading down to the next same/higher-level heading) so they become children of the toggle. Re-parenting recreates those blocks via the API, which means new block IDs and dropped block-scoped comments; use --no-nest to opt out and only flip the toggle flag.
examples: notion pages blocks update BLOCK_ID --text "Updated paragraph text"
notion pages blocks update BLOCK_ID --text "New title" --toggleable
notion pages blocks update BLOCK_ID --json '{"paragraph":{"rich_text":[{"text":{"content":"New text"}}]}}'
notion pages blocks update BLOCK_ID --json-file block_update.json
update.usage_instructions: Run `notion pages blocks update --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --text
options.help: New PLAIN-TEXT content for text-based blocks (paragraph, heading, etc.). Markdown syntax is stored literally; use --markdown/--md-file for links and inline code

options.name: --markdown
options.help: New MARKDOWN content; parses (url) links, `inline code`, **bold**, and *italic* into real Notion rich_text in place

options.name: --md-file
options.help: File containing MARKDOWN content for the block (parsed like --markdown)

options.name: --checked
options.help: Set checked state for to_do blocks

options.name: --json
options.help: Raw JSON block data to update (advanced)

options.name: --json-file
options.help: File containing raw JSON block data to update (advanced)

options.name: --toggleable
options.help: For heading_1/2/3 blocks: turn the toggle arrow on or off

options.name: --no-nest
options.help: When using --toggleable, skip auto-nesting of the heading's section siblings as children. Default behavior moves the blocks between this heading and the next same/higher-level heading into the toggle so the expanded toggle actually shows them

content.usage_instructions: Run `notion pages content --help` for exact arguments and options.

append.help: Append content blocks to a page. Automatically handles content larger than Notion's 100-block limit by splitting into chunks and uploading sequentially.
examples: notion pages content append PAGE_ID --text "Hello world"
notion pages content append PAGE_ID --text "# Heading\n\nParagraph text"
notion pages content append PAGE_ID --file content.md
notion pages content append PAGE_ID --paragraph "Simple paragraph"
notion pages content append PAGE_ID --file chapters.md --is-toggleable
append.usage_instructions: Run `notion pages content append --help` for exact arguments and options.

arguments.name: page_id
arguments.help: The page ID to append content to

options.name: --file
options.help: File containing text/markdown content to append

options.name: --is-toggleable
options.help: Make all heading_1/2/3 blocks generated from markdown into toggle headings

clear.help: Clear all content from a page.
clear.usage_instructions: Run `notion pages content clear --help` for exact arguments and options.

arguments.name: page_id
arguments.help: The page ID to clear content from

replace-section.help: Replace a specific section of a page without touching the rest. Finds the section by its heading text, then replaces all blocks from that heading to (but not including) the next heading at the same or higher level. The heading argument must include the markdown prefix to identify the heading level: '# Title' for H1, '## Title' for H2, '### Title' for H3.
examples: notion pages content replace-section PAGE_ID --heading "## My Section" --file updated.md
notion pages content replace-section PAGE_ID --heading "### Subsection" --text "### Subsection\n\nNew content here"
notion pages content replace-section PAGE_ID --heading "## Old Title" --file new.md --dry-run
replace-section.usage_instructions: Run `notion pages content replace-section --help` for exact arguments and options.

arguments.name: page_id
arguments.help: The page ID containing the section to replace

options.name: --heading
options.help: Section heading to find (e.g., '## My Section Title'). Include the markdown heading prefix (# ## ###)

options.name: --text
options.help: New markdown content to replace the section with

options.name: --file
options.help: File containing new markdown content to replace the section with

options.name: --dry-run
options.help: Show what would be replaced without making changes

set.help: Replace all page content with new content. This clears existing content and sets new content. Automatically handles content larger than Notion's 100-block limit by splitting into chunks and uploading sequentially. Use --json-file to import raw Notion blocks (preserving rich formatting like callouts, colors, columns) exported via 'notion pages export --format notion-json'.
examples: notion pages content set PAGE_ID --text "New content"
notion pages content set PAGE_ID --file content.md
notion pages content set PAGE_ID --json-file blocks.json
notion pages content set PAGE_ID --file outline.md --is-toggleable
set.usage_instructions: Run `notion pages content set --help` for exact arguments and options.

arguments.name: page_id

options.help: File containing text/markdown content to set

options.name: --is-toggleable
options.help: Make all heading_1/2/3 blocks generated from markdown into toggle headings (markdown input only; ignored with --json-file)

examples: notion pages create PARENT_PAGE_ID --title "My New Page"

arguments.help: The parent page ID to create the page under

delete.help: Delete (archive) a page. Note: Notion doesn't truly delete pages - this archives them. Archived pages can be restored using 'notion pages update PAGE_ID --restore'.
delete.usage_instructions: Run `notion pages delete --help` for exact arguments and options.

duplicate.help: Duplicate a page including all blocks and rich formatting. Supports pages in databases and standalone pages (page parents). Preserves column layouts, callouts with colors, bold/italic annotations, images, and all other Notion block types. Handles arbitrary nesting depth.
duplicate.usage_instructions: Run `notion pages duplicate --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --replace
options.help: Find/replace in block text (format: 'old:new'). Can be used multiple times

export.help: Export a Notion page to PDF, HTML, Markdown, or Notion JSON. The notion-json format exports raw Notion block structures preserving all formatting (colors, callouts, columns, bold annotations). The exported JSON can be re-imported with 'blocks append --json-file' or 'content set --json-file'.
examples: notion pages export PAGE_ID -o content.html --format html
notion pages export PAGE_ID -o content.md --format md
notion pages export PAGE_ID -o blocks.json --format notion-json
export.usage_instructions: Run `notion pages export --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --include-blocks
options.help: Include page content blocks in output

import.help: Import a Word document (.docx) as a new Notion page. Converts Word document content to Notion blocks: - Headings → Heading blocks - Paragraphs → Paragraph blocks with formatting (bold, italic, etc.) - Bullet lists → Bulleted list items - Numbered lists → Numbered list items - Tables → Table blocks
examples: notion pages import report.docx -p PARENT_PAGE_ID --title "Q4 Report"
import.usage_instructions: Run `notion pages import --help` for exact arguments and options.

arguments.help: Path to the Word document (.docx) to import

options.help: Page title (defaults to filename without extension)
